story_text.value:
I built r3 to scratch my own itch when reviewing long design doc and planning doc from AI. The chat interface is inherently bad at tracking multiple pieces of feedback across different parts of a document, so I created a lightweight local web UI to organize the review process.

I have been using it on my own projects for one week, and it has made giving structured feedback to AI much easier. I thought others working with AI coding agents might find it useful too.

I'd love to hear what you think and any feedback you have!

title.value: Show HN: R3 – A Local Code Review Tool for You and Your AI Agent

hits.title: GhostApproval: A trust-boundary gap in AI coding assistants

story_text.value:
As Claude Code, Codex, and other AI coding agents take up more and more of our daily work, I've noticed that I now spend most of my time writing specs, nailing down requirements, and talking to the agent, rather than actually poring over the details of the code.

Today, for some totally random reason, it suddenly hit me that I haven't opened Stack Overflow in a very, very long time, at least half a year. And that gives me a strange sense of loss. I used to open it almost every single day. It was there through more than a decade of my career, and somehow I managed to forget about it this fast.

So guys, for how long have you not opened Stack Overflow? And has anything else quietly dropped out of your workflow the same way, without you even noticing?

title.value: Ask HN: How long has it been since you last opened Stack Overflow?

hits.author: novoreorx
